summary:
The available literature provides no evidence regarding trimetazidine's effect on myocardial injury markers after percutaneous coronary intervention, as none of the reviewed studies assess its efficacy in this specific context. Consequently, based on a complete absence of supporting data, trimetazidine is not supported for protecting against myocardial injury post-PCI.  
\boxed{\text{B}}

\boxed{\text{B}}. Answer choice: B
